message:
fix(combinator): convert BulkheadPermit to RAII guard with Drop, fix zombie queue capacity leak

Replace the manual-release BulkheadPermit pattern with a proper RAII guard that
holds a lifetime reference to its parent Bulkhead and releases the permit
automatically via Drop. This eliminates an entire class of permit-leak bugs
where callers could forget to call release_to(), and makes the panic path safe
since Drop runs during unwinding.

Key changes:
- BulkheadPermit<'a> now borrows &'a Bulkhead and implements Drop for automatic release
- API simplified: release_to(bulkhead) → release(self), is_released() removed
- Queue capacity check fixed: count total queue.len() instead of only active entries,
  preventing unbounded memory growth from "zombie" granted-but-never-claimed entries
- call() method uses drop(permit) instead of explicit release for panic safety
- All tests updated for new API; new regression test zombies_fill_queue validates
  that abandoned queue entries correctly count toward the capacity limit
- web/middleware.rs adapted to call p.release() instead of p.release_to(&self.bulkhead)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
